title: Caesalpinia scortechinii
intro: Caesalpinia scortechinii, also known as the "Duku" or "Tulai" tree, is a species of flowering plant in the family Fabaceae. The bark and roots of this plant have been traditionally used in folk medicine to treat various ailments, including fever, rheumatism, and skin conditions, due to its anti-inflammatory, antibacterial, and antifungal properties. The bioactive constituents of Caesalpinia scortechinii include flavonoids, alkaloids, and phenolic acids, which contribute to its medicinal properties. In traditional medicine, the plant is often used to make teas, infusions, and decoctions, as well as being used in the preparation of herbal creams and salves for topical application.
taxonomy: Caesalpinia scortechinii is a species of flowering plant in the family Fabaceae. It is classified in the subfamily Caesalpinioideae, which is a group of legumes that are often found in tropical and subtropical regions. The genus Caesalpinia is a large and diverse group of plants that are native to the Americas, Africa, and Asia. Caesalpinia scortechinii is a tree or shrub that is native to Malaysia and is often found in the wild in the forests of the region. It is a member of the Legume family and is known for its bright yellow flowers and distinctive seed pods.
